meta.note: These are NEW combined-v4 entities that did not have source attribution before

No explicit public statements on AI regulation from AMP PBC or Anjney Midha are present in the available sources. As a major investor in Anthropic — a company that publicly advocates for targeted AI safety regulation — AMP's investment choices may suggest alignment with moderate or targeted regulatory stances, but this is inferred from association rather than explicit statements.
Submitted as "Unknown" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)
Submitted as "Manageable" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)

claims.stance_label: Balanced governance with ethics and accountability
claims.citation: Deregulatory policies could erode accountability mechanisms, increasing the likelihood of deploying high-risk systems without adequate safeguards. Additionally, prioritizing private-sector autonomy over public safeguards may widen societal vulnerabilities and diminish trust in AI systems.

claims.stance_label: Support for safety standards and risk frameworks
claims.citation: Accel AI Institute contributes to the Consortium's efforts along with over two hundred stakeholders through strategic advice, resource sharing, and consulting on ethical AI development for all members. Our focus areas include bias mitigation, explainable AI, and ethical development practices.

claims.stance_label: Call for implementation of existing principles into law
claims.citation: This article is a call to action to refocus priorities, pull back from sci-fi level fears of AI, and understand what is already in place for AI regulation, but not being implemented. How do we get that to happen? What can we as individuals do?
Submitted as "Unknown" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)
Submitted as "Serious" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)

No explicit regulatory stance is stated by the program. Its focus on AI safety research and governance suggests awareness of AI risks, but no specific regulatory positions are articulated in available sources.
Submitted as "Unknown" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)
Submitted as "Unknown" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)

No direct public statements from Captions as a company on AI regulatory stance have been identified in the sources. As an AI-powered consumer product company backed by a16z and angel investors from Snap, the company is focused on product growth rather than policy advocacy, making its regulatory stance unclear.
Submitted as "Unknown" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Inferred from actions)

claims.stance_label: Risks real but manageable through responsible development
claims.citation: AI holds great potential for economic growth as well as individual and societal well-being, but also comes along with risks for human rights, fundamental freedoms, democracy, and the environment. Designing and developing AI in such a way that it serves the good of society and reaching technological sovereignty in AI are therefore of vital importance.

claims.stance_label: Risks require research and mitigation, not prohibition
claims.citation: Viele weitere Potenziale von KI, aber auch die Risiken und wie wir damit umgehen, können nur durch zusätzliche Forschung erkannt werden.

Moreover, it is also about putting the current debate about the risks of AI and necessary legal regulation on a scientifically sound and empirical basis. This also applies to the risk classification of AI systems.

Mit diesem Gesetz setzen wir europäische Vorgaben maximal innovationsoffen um und schaffen eine schlanke KI-Aufsicht mit klarem Blick auf die Bedarfe der Wirtschaft. Wir bauen keine zusätzliche Behörde mit Wasserkopf auf, sondern setzen auf bestehende Strukturen, bewährte Ansprechpartner und bündeln Digital-Expertise bei der Bundesnetzagentur.

entities.name: AMP PBC
entities.notes_preview: AMP PBC is a newly formed venture firm founded by Anjney Midha, a former general partner at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). Midha secured $1.3 billion for AMP's first fund, making it a notable debut in the venture capital landscape. AMP PBC is listed as a significant investor in Anthropic's $30 billion

entities.name: Accel
entities.notes_preview: Accel is a prominent global venture capital firm with a strong focus on technology and AI investments. entities.notes_preview: Athena 2.0 is the second iteration of the Athena research mentorship program, led by Claire Short, focused on fostering diversity of ideas in AI safety research by supporting women and marginalized genders in entering technical and governance AI safety research roles. entities.name: German Federal Government (Bund)
entities.notes_preview: The German Federal Government ('Bund') is the central government of the Federal Republic of Germany, acting as a major funder and policy actor in science, research, and digitalization. entities.name: Captions
entities.notes_preview: Captions (Captions.AI) is an AI-powered creative suite for video content creators, founded by former Snap employees Gaurav Misra and Dwight Churchill. The company was first invested in at the seed stage (February 2021, $4M seed round) by Jacob Andreou as an angel investor and others including Antoin

sources.title: Crossroads in AI Policy: U.S. Shifts and the DeepSeek Global Disruption — Accel.AI
sources.cached_excerpt: The transformative potential of AI necessitates a governance framework that balances innovation with ethical imperatives. Public advocacy, interdisciplinary collaboration, and sustained stakeholder engagement are essential to crafting policies that address the complex interplay of technological advancement and societal impact.

sources.title: The AI Regulation Race - Accel.AI
sources.cached_excerpt: All in all, the Consortium is an important and necessary step in steering a good path forward for AI oversight. However, I anticipate a lot of challenges and needs that we cannot yet anticipate.

sources.title: AI Regulations in the US: Will We See Implementation and Environmental Protection? — Accel.AI
sources.cached_excerpt: This article is a call to action to refocus priorities, pull back from sci-fi level fears of AI, and understand what is already in place for AI regulation, but not being implemented. How do we get that to happen?

sources.title: Kabinett beschließt schlanke KI-Aufsicht in Deutschland
sources.author: Dr. Karsten Wildberger (Bundesdigitalminister)
sources.cached_excerpt: Mit diesem Gesetz setzen wir europäische Vorgaben maximal innovationsoffen um und schaffen eine schlanke KI-Aufsicht mit klarem Blick auf die Bedarfe der Wirtschaft. Wir bauen keine zusätzliche Behörde mit Wasserkopf auf, sondern setzen auf bestehende Strukturen, bewährte Ansprechpartner und bündeln Digital-Expertise bei der Bundesnetzagentur.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence - BMFTR
sources.cached_excerpt: The BMFTR works to defend AI applications against European overregulation and at the same time uses its AI Action Plan to provide new impetus to Germany's AI ecosystem.
